title: linaria glutinosa
intro: Linaria glutinosa, also known as sticky toadflax, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the figwort family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and sore throats. The plant's leaves and flowers contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reduced inflammation and improved wound healing. Herbal preparations of Linaria glutinosa can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to soothe respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
